text.title: AI-Assisted Persona Building Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the ai-assisted-persona-building methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every persona claim (goal, pain, behavior) MUST cite ≥3 evidence items (interview quotes + behavioral signals) keyed by source id.

rationale.source: UX research standards 2026; PAIR design guidelines

rule.rationale: Personas without evidence chain become marketing fluff and break product decisions.

rule.statement: Each persona MUST carry segment-membership rules (observable criteria: plan tier, language, signup channel, behavioral threshold).
rule.rationale: Without explicit rules, personas cannot route product decisions — every PM uses different mental criteria.

rule.statement: Number of personas MUST be ≤5. More requires merge plan.
rule.rationale: Beyond five, personas overlap and product teams revert to ignoring them; the cap forces real segmentation.

rule.statement: Persona spec MUST be signed off by researcher (verified_by) before any downstream use.
rule.rationale: AI-generated personas hallucinate plausibly; verification is the gate against fictional users entering roadmap.

rule.statement: If <8 interviews OR no behavioral data, skip and run a smaller-scale persona effort.
rule.rationale: Methodology assumes data richness; below threshold it produces marketing fluff.
